# caisar.ai Blog v1 Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Ship a minimal personal site + blog at `https://caisar.ai` per `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-07-caisar-ai-blog-design.md` — homepage + `/writing` + individual posts, modern minimal dev aesthetic with dark/light theme, deployed via Cloudflare Pages with custom domain.

**Architecture:** Static site generated by Astro 5 from Markdown/MDX in `src/content/posts/`. Tailwind v4 (CSS-first config, no JS config file) with self-hosted Inter Variable + JetBrains Mono. Cloudflare Pages auto-builds on `git push origin main`; preview deploys on every branch. caisar.ai DNS moves from Porkbun nameservers to Cloudflare nameservers so we can use apex domain + free Web Analytics.

**Tech Stack:**

| Layer        | Version / Choice                                        |
|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------|
| Runtime      | Node.js 22 LTS (matches CF Pages v3 build image default) |
| SSG          | Astro 5+ (`@latest` currently resolves to Astro 6; strict TS default) |
| Styling      | Tailwind v4 via `@tailwindcss/vite` (NOT `@astrojs/tailwind`) |
| Typography plugin | `@tailwindcss/typography` v0.5.19+                  |
| Content      | Markdown + MDX via `@astrojs/mdx`                       |
| Fonts        | `@fontsource-variable/inter` + `@fontsource-variable/jetbrains-mono` |
| Code highlighting | Shiki (built into Astro; dual theme github-light/github-dark) |
| Sitemap      | `@astrojs/sitemap`                                       |
| Hosting      | Cloudflare Pages, free tier                              |
| DNS          | Cloudflare (migrated from Porkbun)                       |
| Analytics    | Cloudflare Web Analytics (auto-injected via proxied DNS) |
| VCS          | Git + GitHub                                             |

**Prerequisites (verify before Task 1):**
- Node.js 22 LTS installed locally (`node --version` → `v22.x`)
- Git installed and configured (`git config --global user.name` set)
- GitHub account with `gh` CLI installed and authenticated (`gh auth status`)
- Cloudflare account (free tier is fine)
- Access to `caisar.ai` domain in Porkbun (logged in via porkbun.com)
- Working directory: `/Users/caisar/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/Caisar/blog`

**Things Cesar needs to provide / decide before launch (per the spec's Open Items):**
- 1–3 sentence bio for the homepage
- Exact social URLs (the plan defaults to LinkedIn + GitHub + `mailto:cesar@caisar.ai`)
- Accent color (the plan defaults to `oklch(0.72 0.17 250)` — a muted blue; tweak the value in `src/styles/global.css` later)
- GitHub repo visibility: public or private (the plan defaults to public)
- The actual first post content (the plan scaffolds a placeholder `hello-world.md`; replace with a real essay before adding the domain to Cloudflare Pages)

## File structure (what we'll create)

```
blog/
├── .gitignore                   # from Astro minimal template
├── .nvmrc                       # pins Node 22 for Cloudflare Pages
├── astro.config.mjs             # mdx + sitemap + tailwindcss vite plugin
├── package.json                 # generated; we install deps
├── tsconfig.json                # from Astro minimal template (extends astro/tsconfigs/strict)
├── README.md                    # one-pager: what this is, how to add a post
├── public/
│   ├── favicon.svg              # simple "C" mark
│   ├── og-default.png           # fallback Open Graph image (placeholder)
│   └── robots.txt               # allow-all + Sitemap: line
└── src/
    ├── content.config.ts        # Zod schema for `posts` collection (NOT src/content/config.ts — v5 path)
    ├── content/posts/
    │   ├── .gitkeep             # ensures the dir tracks even when empty
    │   └── hello-world.mdx      # first post (replace with a real essay)
    ├── components/
    │   ├── BaseHead.astro       # <head>: meta, OG, Twitter, canonical, font preloads, theme-init <script is:inline>
    │   ├── Header.astro         # site name (link to /) + nav (Writing) + ThemeToggle
    │   ├── Footer.astro         # © year + minimal links
    │   ├── ThemeToggle.astro    # sun/moon button + bundled script that flips .dark on <html>
    │   ├── FormattedDate.astro  # <time> element with locale-formatted date
    │   └── PostCard.astro       # date + title + summary + tags in the post list
    ├── layouts/
    │   ├── BaseLayout.astro     # wraps every page: BaseHead + Header + slot + Footer
    │   └── PostLayout.astro     # prose container + reading time + back link
    ├── pages/
    │   ├── index.astro          # homepage
    │   ├── writing/
    │   │   ├── index.astro      # reverse-chronological post list
    │   │   └── [...slug].astro  # individual post (uses PostLayout)
    │   └── 404.astro
    └── styles/
        └── global.css           # @import fonts, @import "tailwindcss", @plugin typography, @custom-variant dark, @theme tokens
```

## Task 1: Scaffold Astro 5 minimal project + git init

**Files:**
- Create: All top-level Astro minimal template files (`astro.config.mjs`, `tsconfig.json`, `package.json`, `.gitignore`, `public/favicon.svg`, `src/pages/index.astro`, etc.) via the CLI
- Create: `.git/` directory
- Create: `.nvmrc` (Node version pin)
- Create: `README.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Verify prerequisites**

Run:
```bash
cd "/Users/caisar/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/Caisar/blog"
node --version    # expect v22.x
git --version     # expect git ≥ 2.x
gh --version      # expect gh ≥ 2.x
ls -la            # expect only `.omc/` and `docs/` subdirectories from earlier work
```

Expected: `node` is on a 22.x LTS. If not, install/switch to Node 22 via nvm (`nvm install 22 && nvm use 22`) before continuing. The directory should be otherwise empty.

- [ ] **Step 2: Scaffold the Astro minimal template into the current directory**

Run:
```bash
npm create astro@latest . -- --template minimal --no-install --no-git --skip-houston --yes
```

Notes on the flags (gotchas the engineer might hit):
- The trailing `.` (dot) is the destination — without it, `create-astro` prompts for a project name and creates a subdirectory.
- The `--` after `astro@latest` is **required** with npm to forward flags to `create-astro`.
- Do NOT add `--typescript strict`. Astro 5 removed the `--typescript` flag entirely; strict TS is the unconditional default.
- `--no-install` skips dependency install (we install ourselves in Task 2).
- `--no-git` skips `git init` (we run it ourselves in Step 4).
- `--yes` suppresses "directory not empty, continue?" since `docs/` and `.omc/` already exist.

Expected after the command: a `package.json`, `astro.config.mjs`, `tsconfig.json`, `.gitignore`, `public/` (with `favicon.svg` **and** `favicon.ico`), and `src/pages/index.astro`. Note: the current minimal template does NOT create a `src/assets/` directory — that's expected, don't go looking for it.

- [ ] **Step 3: Pin the Node version for Cloudflare Pages**

Create file `.nvmrc`:
```
22
```

Cloudflare Pages reads `.nvmrc` as the sole content of the file. We'll also set `NODE_VERSION=22` as an env var in the CF dashboard later (Cloudflare has a known bug where preview branches can ignore production-scoped `NODE_VERSION`; setting `.nvmrc` AND the env var is belt-and-suspenders).

- [ ] **Step 4: Initialize git and make the first commit**

Run:
```bash
git init -b main
git add .
git status   # eyeball — should show all the new template files staged
git commit -m "chore: scaffold Astro 5 minimal template"
```

Expected: `git status` shows files staged including `astro.config.mjs`, `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `.gitignore`, `public/favicon.svg`, `src/pages/index.astro`, `.nvmrc`, plus our existing `docs/`. The commit succeeds.

- [ ] **Step 5: Add a one-pager README**

Create file `README.md`:
````markdown
# caisar.ai

Personal site + blog at https://caisar.ai. See `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-07-caisar-ai-blog-design.md` for design rationale.

## Local development

```bash
npm install      # first time only
npm run dev      # http://localhost:4321
npm run build    # production build to dist/
npm run preview  # serve dist/ locally to validate the prod build
npm run check    # astro check (TS + content schema validation)
```

## Adding a post

1. Create `src/content/posts/<slug>.mdx` with required frontmatter (see schema in `src/content.config.ts`).
2. Write content.
3. `git push origin main` — Cloudflare Pages auto-deploys in ~60-90 seconds.

## Deployment

`main` → production at https://caisar.ai. Any other branch / PR → preview URL at `https://<branch>.<project>.pages.dev`.
````

- [ ] **Step 6: Commit**

```bash
git add .nvmrc README.md
git commit -m "chore: pin Node 22 + add README"
```

## Task 2: Install all dependencies

**Files:**
- Modify: `package.json` (npm adds the new deps)
- Create: `package-lock.json`
- Create: `node_modules/`

- [ ] **Step 1: Install runtime + dev dependencies**

Run:
```bash
# Astro template's astro dep + integrations + Tailwind v4 stack + fonts:
npm install \
  @astrojs/mdx@latest \
  @astrojs/sitemap@latest \
  @tailwindcss/vite@latest \
  tailwindcss@latest \
  @fontsource-variable/inter@latest \
  @fontsource-variable/jetbrains-mono@latest

# Dev-only — typography plugin + the tooling `astro check` needs:
npm install -D @tailwindcss/typography@latest @astrojs/check@latest typescript@latest

# Add a `check` script (the minimal template's package.json doesn't include one):
npm pkg set scripts.check="astro check"
```

Notes:
- Do NOT install `@astrojs/tailwind` — that integration is Tailwind v3 only and is now legacy in Astro's docs.
- `tailwindcss` and `@tailwindcss/vite` must share the same major.minor version; installing both with `@latest` ensures that.
- `@tailwindcss/typography` ≥ 0.5.16 is required for v4's `@plugin` directive — `@latest` resolves to 0.5.19+ at time of writing.
- `@astrojs/check` + `typescript` are required by `npx astro check` (used as the verification gate in nearly every task). Installing them up front avoids the interactive "install @astrojs/check?" prompt the first time you run `astro check` — which would otherwise stall a non-interactive run.
- `npm pkg set` edits `package.json` non-interactively so `npm run check` works (the README references it).

Expected: `npm install` completes without errors. `package.json` now lists the deps.

- [ ] **Step 2: Sanity check the dep versions**

Run:
```bash
node -e "const p = require('./package.json'); console.log(JSON.stringify({deps: p.dependencies, dev: p.devDependencies}, null, 2));"
```

Expected output should include (versions may differ):
```json
{
  "deps": {
    "astro": "^5.x.x or ^6.x.x",
    "@astrojs/mdx": "^x.x.x",
    "@astrojs/sitemap": "^x.x.x",

    "@fontsource-variable/inter": "...",
    "@fontsource-variable/jetbrains-mono": "..."

  "dev": {
    "@tailwindcss/typography": "^0.5.x",
    "@astrojs/check": "^x.x.x",
    "typescript": "^5.x.x"
  }
}
```

Versions for `tailwindcss` and `@tailwindcss/vite` should be the same major.minor. Also confirm `package.json` now has a `check` script:
```bash
node -e "console.log(require('./package.json').scripts)"
```
Expected: an object containing `dev`, `build`, `preview`, `astro`, AND `check`.

## Task 3: Configure astro.config.mjs

**Files:**
- Modify: `astro.config.mjs`

- [ ] **Step 1: Replace the scaffolded config with the production config**

Overwrite `astro.config.mjs` with:

```js
// @ts-check
import { defineConfig } from 'astro/config';
import mdx from '@astrojs/mdx';
import sitemap from '@astrojs/sitemap';
import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite';

// https://astro.build/config
export default defineConfig({
  site: 'https://caisar.ai',
  integrations: [
    mdx(),
    sitemap({
      // Exclude the 404 page. The filter receives the FULL URL. Astro emits
      // 404 as a special file (dist/404.html), so its sitemap URL may be /404
      // WITH OR WITHOUT a trailing slash depending on build format — use a
      // substring check rather than a fragile exact-equality match.
      filter: (page) => !page.includes('/404'),
    }),
  ],
  markdown: {
    // Shiki dual themes with class-based switching. defaultColor:false makes
    // Shiki emit ONLY CSS variables (no baked-in inline colors), so the `.dark`
    // class on <html> fully controls which theme renders. The matching CSS
    // lives in src/styles/global.css (section 7). Without this block Astro
    // defaults to a SINGLE theme (github-dark) and code blocks won't switch —
    // which would fail acceptance criterion 4.
    shikiConfig: {
      themes: {
        light: 'github-light',
        dark: 'github-dark',
      },
      defaultColor: false,
    },
  },
  vite: {
    plugins: [tailwindcss()],
  },
});
```

Key things to know:
- The `site` field is **required** by `@astrojs/sitemap` — without it, the sitemap integration cannot generate the XML.
- MDX goes in `integrations: []`. Tailwind v4 goes in `vite.plugins: []`. They never conflict because they live in different config sections.
- Do NOT add `import tailwind from '@astrojs/tailwind'` — that's the deprecated v3 integration.
- The `markdown.shikiConfig` block is what makes code blocks theme-aware. `defaultColor: false` is non-negotiable for class-based switching: with the default (`'light'`), Shiki bakes light colors inline and you'd need `!important` to override. With `false`, Shiki emits only `--shiki-light*` / `--shiki-dark*` CSS variables and the CSS in Task 4 (section 7) drives both states.

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify the config parses**

Run:
```bash
npx astro sync
```

Expected: command succeeds. `astro sync` is the cheapest way to verify the config (it parses `astro.config.mjs` and regenerates `.astro/` types). If it fails, the error message points at the syntax issue.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add astro.config.mjs
git commit -m "feat: configure astro.config.mjs with mdx, sitemap, tailwind v4"
```

## Task 4: Configure global.css (Tailwind v4, fonts, dark variant, theme tokens)

**Files:**
- Create: `src/styles/global.css`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the global stylesheet**

Create `src/styles/global.css` with:

```css
/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 * 1. Fontsource imports — MUST come first.
 *    CSS @import order is strict: all @import statements must precede any
 *    other CSS rule, or they are ignored per spec.
 *    The bare specifier resolves to the wght axis file (weights 100-900 in
 *    one small woff2), which is what we want for body + headings.
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */
@import "@fontsource-variable/inter";
@import "@fontsource-variable/jetbrains-mono";

/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 * 2. Tailwind v4 single-line import.
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */
@import "tailwindcss";

/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 * 3. Plugins are registered in CSS in v4 (no tailwind.config.js needed).
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */
@plugin "@tailwindcss/typography";

/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 * 4. Dark variant.
 *    Tailwind v4 removed the JS `darkMode: 'class'` option. Without this
 *    @custom-variant line, `dark:` utilities only respond to
 *    prefers-color-scheme and the manual toggle silently does nothing.
 *    The :where() wrapper keeps specificity at 0 so utility ordering wins.
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */
@custom-variant dark (&:where(.dark, .dark *));

/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 * 5. Design tokens.
 *    Anything declared in @theme becomes both a CSS variable AND a utility
 *    class (e.g. --color-accent → bg-accent / text-accent / border-accent).
 *    @theme blocks must be top-level — never nested under :root or a media
 *    query (Tailwind enforces this at parse time).
 *
 *    The font family name is "Inter Variable" (with the Variable suffix) —
 *    that's what Fontsource registers in the @font-face src. Using plain
 *    "Inter" silently falls back to system fonts. Same for JetBrains Mono.
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */
@theme {
  --font-sans: "Inter Variable", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system,
               "Segoe UI", Roboto, "Helvetica Neue", Arial, sans-serif;
  --font-mono: "JetBrains Mono Variable", ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular,
               Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, "Liberation Mono", monospace;

  /* Muted blue accent. Adjust to taste in Open Items follow-up.
   * oklch() is the v4-idiomatic wide-gamut color space. */
  --color-accent: oklch(0.55 0.13 250);
  --color-accent-fg: oklch(0.98 0.01 250);
}

/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 * 6. Base layer overrides.
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */
@layer base {
  /* Make sans the document default so unstyled elements pick it up. */
  html {
    font-family: var(--font-sans);
    /* Native form controls + scrollbars follow the theme. Tailwind v4 does
     * not set color-scheme automatically in class mode. */
    color-scheme: light dark;
  }
  /* Stop Safari's default 16px text-size bump on focus inside <input>. */
  html { -webkit-text-size-adjust: 100%; }
  code, pre, kbd, samp { font-family: var(--font-mono); }
}

/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 * 7. Shiki dual-theme code blocks (class-based switching).
 *    astro.config.mjs (Task 3) sets shikiConfig.themes { light, dark } +
 *    defaultColor:false, so Shiki emits ONLY CSS variables (no baked-in
 *    colors). We therefore MUST style BOTH the default (light) state and the
 *    .dark state — a lone html.dark rule would leave light mode unstyled.
 *    Astro's wrapper class is .astro-code (its rename of Shiki's .shiki).
 *    Token color/font vars live on the child <span>s; background vars live on
 *    the <pre>. We override color, background, AND the three emphasis vars so
 *    italic/bold/underlined tokens track the active theme.
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */
.astro-code,
.astro-code span {
  color: var(--shiki-light);
  background-color: var(--shiki-light-bg);
  font-style: var(--shiki-light-font-style);
  font-weight: var(--shiki-light-font-weight);
  text-decoration: var(--shiki-light-text-decoration);
}

html.dark .astro-code,
html.dark .astro-code span {
  color: var(--shiki-dark);
  background-color: var(--shiki-dark-bg);
  font-style: var(--shiki-dark-font-style);
  font-weight: var(--shiki-dark-font-weight);
  text-decoration: var(--shiki-dark-text-decoration);
}

/* @tailwindcss/typography `prose pre` ships its own background that would
 * repaint over Shiki's <pre>. Re-assert Shiki's bg on the code block in both
 * themes so the github-light / github-dark backgrounds show through. */
.prose pre.astro-code {
  background-color: var(--shiki-light-bg);
}
html.dark .prose pre.astro-code {
  background-color: var(--shiki-dark-bg);
}
```

Notes on each numbered block (so the engineer understands why):
- **1 (Fontsource imports first)**: CSS spec requires `@import` rules to precede any other rule. If you put them after `@import "tailwindcss";` they're ignored at runtime and the fonts silently don't load.
- **2 (Tailwind import)**: pulls in the v4 core. No separate `@tailwind base/components/utilities` lines — that was v3.
- **3 (`@plugin`)**: v4's CSS-first way to register plugins. There's no `tailwind.config.js` at all.
- **4 (dark variant)**: this is the single most error-prone line in Tailwind v4 setups. Skipping it means `dark:` only follows the OS preference; the toggle button does nothing.
- **5 (`@theme`)**: setting `--font-sans` and `--color-accent` regenerates the corresponding utilities. The `oklch()` accent is the v4 idiom.
- **6 (base layer)**: sets the document font and tells the browser to draw form controls in the current theme.

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify Tailwind compiles**

We can't fully verify until a page imports this CSS (Task 10), but we can verify the file syntactically:

```bash
npx astro sync
```

Expected: succeeds (no parse error from the CSS yet — that comes at build time, which we'll exercise later).

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add src/styles/global.css
git commit -m "feat: add Tailwind v4 global.css with fonts, dark variant, theme tokens"
```

## Task 5: Define the content collection schema

**Files:**
- Create: `src/content.config.ts`
- Create: `src/content/posts/.gitkeep` (so the directory exists in git)

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the collection config**

Create `src/content.config.ts` (NOT `src/content/config.ts` — Astro 5 moved this path):

```ts
import { defineCollection } from 'astro:content';
import { z } from 'astro/zod';
import { glob } from 'astro/loaders';

const posts = defineCollection({
  loader: glob({
    pattern: '**/*.{md,mdx}',
    base: './src/content/posts',
  }),
  // Use the function-form schema so we can access the image() helper.
  schema: ({ image }) =>
    z.object({
      title: z.string().max(120),
      summary: z.string().max(280),
      pubDate: z.coerce.date(),
      updated: z.coerce.date().optional(),
      tags: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
      draft: z.boolean().default(false),
      hero: image().optional(),
    }),
});

export const collections = { posts };
```

Key things to verify if the engineer copies this:
- Import `z` from `'astro/zod'`, NOT from `'astro:content'`. `npm create astro@latest` now resolves to **Astro 6**, where `z` re-exported from `astro:content` is deprecated (console warning) and slated for removal in Astro 7. `astro/zod` is the forward-compatible source. (If you pinned to Astro 5, `import { defineCollection, z } from 'astro:content'` also works — but `astro/zod` is correct on both.)
- The loader import is `from 'astro/loaders'` (slash, NOT colon — `astro:loaders` does not exist).
- File path is `src/content.config.ts`, NOT `src/content/config.ts`. The legacy v4 path still works as a fallback but is no longer canonical.
- `z.coerce.date()` (not `z.date()`) — YAML frontmatter dates parse as strings; coerce is required.
- Function-form schema `schema: ({ image }) =>` — the plain `z.object({...})` form does NOT have access to the `image()` helper.

- [ ] **Step 2: Create the content directory placeholder**

Run:
```bash
mkdir -p src/content/posts
touch src/content/posts/.gitkeep
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Regenerate types and verify**

```bash
npx astro sync
npx astro check
```

Expected: both commands succeed. `astro sync` regenerates `.astro/types.d.ts` so `CollectionEntry<'posts'>` becomes typed. `astro check` reports 0 errors, 0 warnings (it'll warn-but-not-error about the empty collection).

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add src/content.config.ts src/content/posts/.gitkeep
git commit -m "feat: define posts content collection with Zod schema"
```

## Task 6: BaseHead component (meta tags, fonts, theme-init script)

**Files:**
- Create: `src/components/BaseHead.astro`

This is the single most subtle component in the project — it bundles together five concerns that all live in `<head>`:
1. Meta tags (charset, viewport, description, canonical).
2. Open Graph + Twitter card tags for link previews.
3. Stylesheet import (`global.css`).
4. Font preload hints.
5. The blocking inline theme-init script (no-flash dark mode).

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the component**

Create `src/components/BaseHead.astro`:

```astro
---
import "../styles/global.css";
import interWoff2 from "@fontsource-variable/inter/files/inter-latin-wght-normal.woff2?url";
import jetbrainsWoff2 from "@fontsource-variable/jetbrains-mono/files/jetbrains-mono-latin-wght-normal.woff2?url";

interface Props {
  title: string;
  description: string;
  /** Path to OG image, relative to site root. Defaults to /og-default.png */
  ogImage?: string;
  /** ISO date string for article:published_time */
  pubDate?: string;
}

const { title, description, ogImage = "/og-default.png", pubDate } = Astro.props;
const canonical = new URL(Astro.url.pathname, Astro.site).toString();
const ogImageUrl = new URL(ogImage, Astro.site).toString();
---

<!-- ------------------------------------------------------------------------
  Blocking inline theme-init script.
  is:inline is non-negotiable — without it Astro hoists/bundles the script as
  type="module" (deferred), so the .dark class would be added AFTER first paint,
  which is exactly the flash we're killing. As a render-blocking inline script
  it executes synchronously before the browser paints, so it sets the theme on
  <html> in time regardless of where Astro places the stylesheet <link> in the
  final <head>.
------------------------------------------------------------------------- -->
<script is:inline>
  (function () {
    try {
      const stored = localStorage.getItem("theme");
      const prefersDark = window.matchMedia("(prefers-color-scheme: dark)").matches;
      const isDark = stored === "dark" || (!stored && prefersDark);
      document.documentElement.classList.toggle("dark", isDark);
      document.documentElement.dataset.theme = isDark ? "dark" : "light";
    } catch (_) {
      if (window.matchMedia("(prefers-color-scheme: dark)").matches) {
        document.documentElement.classList.add("dark");
        document.documentElement.dataset.theme = "dark";
      }
    }
  })();
</script>

<!-- Basic meta -->
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
<meta name="generator" content={Astro.generator} />
<link rel="icon" type="image/svg+xml" href="/favicon.svg" />
<link rel="canonical" href={canonical} />

<!-- Font preload hints. Vite's ?url suffix produces a hashed asset URL in prod. -->
<link rel="preload" as="font" type="font/woff2" href={interWoff2} crossorigin="anonymous" />
<link rel="preload" as="font" type="font/woff2" href={jetbrainsWoff2} crossorigin="anonymous" />

<!-- Page meta -->
<title>{title}</title>
<meta name="description" content={description} />

<!-- Open Graph -->
<meta property="og:type" content={pubDate ? "article" : "website"} />
<meta property="og:title" content={title} />
<meta property="og:description" content={description} />
<meta property="og:url" content={canonical} />
<meta property="og:image" content={ogImageUrl} />
{pubDate && <meta property="article:published_time" content={pubDate} />}

<!-- Twitter card -->
<meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image" />
<meta name="twitter:title" content={title} />
<meta name="twitter:description" content={description} />
<meta name="twitter:image" content={ogImageUrl} />
```

Why this shape (notes for the engineer):
- The theme-init `<script is:inline>` is render-blocking, so it runs synchronously before the browser's first paint and sets the `dark` class on `<html>` in time — regardless of the exact order Astro emits the stylesheet `<link>` in `<head>`. This eliminates the flash-of-incorrect-theme. (Astro hoists CSS imports into `<head>`, so don't expect the `<link>` to literally come after this script in the output; it doesn't need to.)
- Both `localStorage.getItem('theme')` and `matchMedia('prefers-color-scheme: dark')` are checked. The user's explicit choice wins; OS preference is the fallback. The try/catch handles Safari Private Mode / embedded webviews that throw on `localStorage` access.
- Setting `dataset.theme` in addition to `classList` is belt-and-suspenders: Tailwind reads `.dark`, third-party libs (Shiki dual-theme) read `[data-theme]`.
- `?url` on the woff2 imports: Vite emits hashed asset URLs in prod; hard-coded `/node_modules/...` paths break.
- `Astro.site` is set in `astro.config.mjs` (Task 3) — it's required for absolute canonical/OG URLs.

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify the preloaded font files actually exist at those paths**

The two `?url` font imports hard-reference specific woff2 filenames. If Fontsource ever renames the `latin-wght-normal` file in a newer release, the import fails the build. Confirm they resolve:

```bash
ls node_modules/@fontsource-variable/inter/files/ | grep 'latin-wght-normal'
ls node_modules/@fontsource-variable/jetbrains-mono/files/ | grep 'latin-wght-normal'
```

Expected: each command prints `inter-latin-wght-normal.woff2` / `jetbrains-mono-latin-wght-normal.woff2`. If the filename differs, update the two `import ... from "...?url"` lines in `BaseHead.astro` to match the actual file.

- [ ] **Step 3: Sanity check that the component parses**

Don't worry about rendering yet — we'll wire it through `BaseLayout` in Task 11. For now, just verify the file parses:

```bash
npx astro check
```

Expected: 0 errors. (Warnings about the unused component are fine.)

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add src/components/BaseHead.astro
git commit -m "feat: add BaseHead component (meta, OG, fonts, no-flash theme init)"
```

## Task 8: ThemeToggle component

**Files:**
- Create: `src/components/ThemeToggle.astro`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the component**

Create `src/components/ThemeToggle.astro`:

```astro
---
// Renders a sun/moon button. The bundled <script> below wires click → flip class
// on <html>. The inline init script in BaseHead.astro has already set the
// correct initial state before this component renders, so there is no flash.
---
<button
  id="theme-toggle"
  type="button"
  aria-label="Toggle color theme"
  class="inline-flex h-8 w-8 items-center justify-center rounded-md text-zinc-600 transition-colors hover:bg-zinc-100 hover:text-accent dark:text-zinc-400 dark:hover:bg-zinc-800"
>
  <!-- Moon (shown in light mode) -->
  <svg
    class="h-4 w-4 dark:hidden"
    viewBox="0 0 24 24"
    fill="none"
    stroke="currentColor"
    stroke-width="2"
    stroke-linecap="round"
    stroke-linejoin="round"
    aria-hidden="true"
  >
    <path d="M21 12.79A9 9 0 1 1 11.21 3 7 7 0 0 0 21 12.79z" />
  </svg>
  <!-- Sun (shown in dark mode) -->
  <svg
    class="hidden h-4 w-4 dark:block"
    viewBox="0 0 24 24"
    fill="none"
    stroke="currentColor"
    stroke-width="2"
    stroke-linecap="round"
    stroke-linejoin="round"
    aria-hidden="true"
  >
    <circle cx="12" cy="12" r="4" />
    <path d="M12 2v2M12 20v2M4.93 4.93l1.41 1.41M17.66 17.66l1.41 1.41M2 12h2M20 12h2M6.34 17.66l-1.41 1.41M19.07 4.93l-1.41 1.41" />
  </svg>
</button>

<script>
  const apply = (theme: "dark" | "light") => {
    const isDark = theme === "dark";
    document.documentElement.classList.toggle("dark", isDark);
    document.documentElement.dataset.theme = theme;
  };

  const current = (): "dark" | "light" =>
    document.documentElement.classList.contains("dark") ? "dark" : "light";

  const wire = () => {
    const btn = document.getElementById("theme-toggle");
    if (!btn || btn.dataset.wired) return;
    btn.dataset.wired = "true";
    btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
      const next = current() === "dark" ? "light" : "dark";
      apply(next);
      try {
        localStorage.setItem("theme", next);
      } catch (_) {
        // Safari private mode / embedded webview; persistence not available.
      }
    });
  };

  wire();
</script>
```

Why icons in this order: the `hidden h-4 w-4 dark:block` class on the sun comes AFTER `dark:hidden h-4 w-4` on the moon in source order. With `:where(.dark, .dark *)` keeping specificity at 0, source order is what decides the cascade. Don't swap them — the moon would show in dark mode.


Expected: 0 errors.

```bash
git commit -m "feat: add ThemeToggle with sun/moon icons and persisted preference"
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create PostCard.astro**

Create `src/components/PostCard.astro`:

```astro
---
import type { CollectionEntry } from "astro:content";
import FormattedDate from "./FormattedDate.astro";

interface Props {
  post: CollectionEntry<"posts">;
}
const { post } = Astro.props;
const href = `/writing/${post.id}/`;
---

<article class="py-5 border-b border-zinc-200 last:border-b-0 dark:border-zinc-800">
  <a href={href} class="group block">
    <FormattedDate
      date={post.data.pubDate}
      class="block text-xs text-zinc-500 dark:text-zinc-400"
    />
    <h2 class="mt-1 text-base font-semibold tracking-tight text-zinc-900 group-hover:text-accent dark:text-zinc-100">
      {post.data.title}
    </h2>
    <p class="mt-1 text-sm text-zinc-600 dark:text-zinc-400">
      {post.data.summary}
    </p>
    {post.data.tags.length > 0 && (
      <ul class="mt-2 flex flex-wrap gap-1.5">
        {post.data.tags.map((tag) => (
          <li class="text-xs text-zinc-500 dark:text-zinc-500">#{tag}</li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    )}
  </a>
</article>
```

Key things:
- `post.id` (NOT `post.slug`) — Astro 5 v5 content layer dropped the separate `.slug` property.
- The trailing slash on `href` matches Astro's default `trailingSlash: 'ignore'` output and what the sitemap will emit (so URLs match for the 404 filter).

## Task 11: PostLayout

**Files:**
- Create: `src/layouts/PostLayout.astro`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the layout**

Create `src/layouts/PostLayout.astro`:

```astro
---
import BaseLayout from "./BaseLayout.astro";
import FormattedDate from "../components/FormattedDate.astro";
import type { CollectionEntry } from "astro:content";

interface Props {
  post: CollectionEntry<"posts">;
  readingTime?: string;
}

const { post, readingTime } = Astro.props;
const { title, summary, pubDate, updated, tags } = post.data;
---

<BaseLayout
  title={title}
  description={summary}
  pubDate={pubDate.toISOString()}
>
  <article>
    <header class="mb-8">
      <h1 class="text-2xl font-semibold tracking-tight text-zinc-900 dark:text-zinc-100">
        {title}
      </h1>
      <div class="mt-2 flex items-center gap-2 text-xs text-zinc-500 dark:text-zinc-400">
        <FormattedDate date={pubDate} />
        {updated && (
          <>
            <span aria-hidden="true">·</span>
            <span>Updated <FormattedDate date={updated} /></span>
          </>
        )}
        {readingTime && (
          <>
            <span aria-hidden="true">·</span>
            <span>{readingTime}</span>
          </>
        )}
      </div>
      {tags.length > 0 && (
        <ul class="mt-3 flex flex-wrap gap-1.5">
          {tags.map((tag) => (
            <li class="text-xs text-zinc-500 dark:text-zinc-500">#{tag}</li>
          ))}
        </ul>
      )}
    </header>

    <div
      class="prose prose-zinc max-w-none dark:prose-invert
             prose-headings:font-semibold prose-headings:tracking-tight
             prose-a:text-accent prose-a:no-underline hover:prose-a:underline
             prose-code:font-normal prose-code:before:hidden prose-code:after:hidden"
    >
      <slot />
    </div>

    <footer class="mt-16 border-t border-zinc-200 pt-6 dark:border-zinc-800">
      <a
        href="/writing/"
        class="text-sm text-zinc-600 hover:text-accent dark:text-zinc-400"
      >
        ← Back to writing
      </a>
    </footer>
  </article>
</BaseLayout>
```

Notes:
- The `prose prose-zinc dark:prose-invert` chain is Tailwind Typography v4 syntax (loaded as a plugin in `global.css`).
- We override `prose-code:before:hidden prose-code:after:hidden` to kill the default backtick-as-pseudo-element that Typography adds around inline code (looks ugly with our minimal aesthetic).
- We deliberately do NOT set `prose-pre:bg-*` here. Shiki owns the `<pre>` background via the dual-theme CSS variables (Task 4, section 7). Adding a Tailwind `prose-pre` background would repaint over Shiki's github-light/github-dark backgrounds and break theme-aware code blocks.
- We pass `pubDate.toISOString()` to BaseLayout so the `article:published_time` Open Graph tag is correct.

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify**

```bash
npx astro check
```

Expected: 0 errors.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash

git commit -m "feat: add PostLayout with prose container, dates, tags, back link"
```

## Task 14: /writing/[...slug] dynamic post page

**Files:**
- Create: `src/pages/writing/[...slug].astro`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the page**

Create `src/pages/writing/[...slug].astro`:

```astro
---
import { getCollection, render, type CollectionEntry } from "astro:content";
import PostLayout from "../../layouts/PostLayout.astro";

export async function getStaticPaths() {
  const posts = await getCollection("posts", ({ data }) => {
    return import.meta.env.PROD ? data.draft !== true : true;
  });
  return posts.map((post) => ({
    params: { slug: post.id },
    props: { post },
  }));
}

interface Props {
  post: CollectionEntry<"posts">;
}

const { post } = Astro.props;
const { Content, remarkPluginFrontmatter } = await render(post);

// Compute a cheap reading-time string from word count if the remark plugin
// hasn't injected one. (We don't add a plugin in v1 — just count words.)
const wordCount = post.body?.trim().split(/\s+/).length ?? 0;
const minutes = Math.max(1, Math.round(wordCount / 220));
const readingTime = remarkPluginFrontmatter?.readingTime ?? `${minutes} min read`;
---

<PostLayout post={post} readingTime={readingTime}>
  <Content />
</PostLayout>
```

Critical v5 syntax to verify if copied:
- `import { render } from 'astro:content'` and `await render(post)` — NOT `post.render()` (that was v4).
- `entry.id` is what fills `params.slug` (NOT `entry.slug`, which doesn't exist in v5).
- `post.body` is the raw markdown source string; safe to count words on.

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify**

```bash
npx astro check
```

Expected: 0 errors.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add src/pages/writing/[...slug].astro
git commit -m "feat: add /writing/[...slug] dynamic post page"
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Add a placeholder OG image**

We need `public/og-default.png` so Open Graph link previews work from day one. Quick path: generate a 1200×630 PNG with the text "caisar.ai" centered, dark background. Use ImageMagick if installed:

```bash
# Option A: ImageMagick (preferred)
if command -v magick >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  magick -size 1200x630 xc:'#0c0a09' \
    -fill '#fafaf9' \
    -gravity center -pointsize 80 -font Helvetica-Bold \
    -annotate +0+0 'caisar.ai' \
    public/og-default.png
else
  echo "Install ImageMagick (brew install imagemagick) or download a 1200x630 placeholder PNG manually."
fi
```

If ImageMagick is not installed, the engineer should either install it (`brew install imagemagick` on macOS) and re-run, or temporarily download any 1200×630 PNG and save it to `public/og-default.png`. Replace with a real image before launch.

Verify the file exists and is ~1200×630:
```bash
file public/og-default.png  # should report PNG image
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Create robots.txt**

Create `public/robots.txt`:

```
User-agent: *
Allow: /

Sitemap: https://caisar.ai/sitemap-index.xml
```

`@astrojs/sitemap` does NOT generate `robots.txt`, so we must add the `Sitemap:` line ourselves.

- [ ] **Step 4: Verify**

```bash
ls -la public/
```

Expected: `favicon.svg`, `og-default.png`, `robots.txt` all present.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
# -A so the favicon.ico deletion is staged alongside the new files.
git add -A public/
git commit -m "feat: add favicon, OG default image, robots.txt; drop template favicon.ico"
```

## Task 17: First post (hello world placeholder)

**Files:**
- Create: `src/content/posts/hello-world.mdx`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create a placeholder first post**

Create `src/content/posts/hello-world.mdx` (NOTE: this plan-fence uses **four** backticks because the file itself contains a triple-backtick code block):

````mdx
---
title: "Hello, world"
summary: "First post. A note about what this site is and what I'll be writing here."
pubDate: 2026-06-07
tags: ["meta"]
draft: false
---

This is the first post on `caisar.ai`. I'm planning to write about AI,
infrastructure, digital sovereignty, and the politics of AI policy — the
kinds of things I think about during the day and don't have a great place
to put them otherwise.

A few quick notes on what to expect:

- **Cadence:** irregular. I'd rather publish three good essays a year than
  twenty mediocre ones.
- **Format:** essays, not link-blog updates. If a thought fits in a tweet,
  it doesn't belong here.
- **Topics:** AI capabilities and deployment, infrastructure economics,
  EU digital sovereignty, and the politics around all of the above.

If you want to read more — or argue — you can find me by email
(`cesar@caisar.ai`).

```py
def main():
    print("hello, world")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```
````

Notes:
- This post is a real first post Cesar should rewrite (or replace) before launching. The structure works as a template: required frontmatter, prose, a code block to validate Shiki highlighting.
- The fenced code block (` ```py `) tests Shiki dual-theme rendering. Verify it renders with syntax colors in both themes.

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify build picks it up**

Run the dev server and visit `http://localhost:4321/writing/`:

```bash
npm run dev
```

Open `http://localhost:4321/writing/` in a browser. Expected: the post appears in the list. Click it → `http://localhost:4321/writing/hello-world/` renders the post with the code block syntax-highlighted, dates visible, theme toggle works without flash.

Kill the dev server with Ctrl+C.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add src/content/posts/hello-world.mdx
git commit -m "content: add hello world placeholder post"
```

## Task 18: Local verification — full production build, preview, and Lighthouse

**Files:** none (verification only)

- [ ] **Step 1: Type-check and content-schema validation**

```bash
npx astro check
```

Expected: `0 errors, 0 warnings`. If there are errors, fix them before continuing.

- [ ] **Step 2: Production build**

```bash
npm run build
```

Expected output (last few lines):
- `▶ /` (homepage)
- `▶ /writing/` (writing index)
- `▶ /writing/hello-world/` (post)
- `▶ /404` (404)
- `▶ /sitemap-index.xml` and `▶ /sitemap-0.xml` (sitemap)
- "Complete!" / no errors

Inspect the output:
```bash
ls dist/
ls dist/writing/
cat dist/sitemap-index.xml      # should reference sitemap-0.xml
cat dist/sitemap-0.xml          # should list /, /writing/, /writing/hello-world/ but NOT /404/
cat dist/robots.txt             # should be our file
ls dist/_astro/                 # should contain hashed CSS/JS bundles
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Serve the prod build and smoke test**

```bash
npm run preview
```

Open `http://localhost:4321` and verify, in this order:

1. **Homepage** (`/`):
   - Loads without flash of incorrect theme.
   - Header shows "Cesar" + Writing + theme toggle button.
   - Bio paragraph renders in Inter (sans).
   - "Latest writing" shows the hello-world post.
   - Footer present.
2. **Theme toggle**:
   - Click the sun/moon — page flips light↔dark. Reload → preference persists.
   - Open DevTools → Application → Local Storage → confirm `theme: dark` (or `light`) is set.
3. **`/writing`**:
   - Lists hello-world post with date, title, summary, tag.
4. **`/writing/hello-world/`**:
   - Title, date, "1 min read" or similar.
   - Code block renders with syntax colors.
   - **Toggle the theme while viewing the post** — the code block's colors AND background must switch between github-light and github-dark. (If they don't, the Shiki dual-theme config from Task 3 or the CSS from Task 4 section 7 is missing/wrong.)
   - "← Back to writing" link at bottom works.
5. **404** (`/some/random/path`):
   - Shows our custom 404 page.
6. **Network tab**:
   - No 404s on fonts, CSS, or JS.
   - `Inter-Variable.woff2` (or similar) loads with `priority: high` (preload working).
7. **View source** on the homepage:
   - The inline theme-init script (`(function () { ... localStorage.getItem('theme') ... })()`) is present in `<head>`. (Exact position relative to the stylesheet `<link>` doesn't matter — it's render-blocking.)
   - `<meta property="og:image">` points to `https://caisar.ai/og-default.png`.
   - `<link rel="canonical">` points to `https://caisar.ai/`.

- [ ] **Step 4: Lighthouse audit**

In Chrome DevTools → Lighthouse tab → Mobile + Performance + Accessibility + Best Practices + SEO → Analyze.

Run it on all three URL types (matches acceptance criterion 6): the homepage (`/`), the writing index (`/writing/`), AND a post (`/writing/hello-world/`).

Expected: ≥95 on Performance, Accessibility, Best Practices, and SEO on all three.

If scores are low, common culprits:
- LCP slow → preload not working (check the woff2 URL is hashed).
- CLS > 0 → font swap shifting; should be minimal since we preload.
- Best Practices < 95 → check the console for errors.

Document any Lighthouse failures by category here before fixing.

Kill the preview server with Ctrl+C.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit any fixes from Lighthouse iteration**

If Lighthouse caught real issues, fix them and commit:
```bash
git add <fixed files>
git commit -m "fix: address Lighthouse findings"
```

If no fixes needed, skip this step.

Default: **public** (per spec recommendation). If Cesar wants private, replace `--public` with `--private`.

```bash
gh repo create caisar-ai \
  --public \

```

This single command:
- Creates `github.com/<your-username>/caisar-ai` (public).
- Adds it as the `origin` remote.
- Pushes the current `main` branch.

Expected: command finishes with the new repo URL printed. Open it in a browser to verify.

```bash
git remote -v
git branch -vv
```

Expected: `origin` set to the GitHub URL; `main` branch shows `[origin/main]` tracking info.

## Task 20: Connect Cloudflare Pages to the GitHub repo

**Files:** none (all manual dashboard work)

This and the next two tasks are **manual UI work** in the Cloudflare dashboard and Porkbun. They have no shell commands beyond verification — follow each step precisely.

- [ ] **Step 1: Log in to Cloudflare**

Go to https://dash.cloudflare.com and log in.

- [ ] **Step 2: Open Workers & Pages**

In the left sidebar, click **Workers & Pages** (the old standalone "Pages" sidebar entry no longer exists; everything is under Workers & Pages now).

- [ ] **Step 3: Create a Pages project**

1. Click **Create application** (top right).
2. Click the **Pages** tab.
3. Click **Connect to Git**.
4. Click **Connect GitHub** and complete the OAuth flow. On the GitHub side, click **Install & Authorize**. Either choose **All repositories** or select only `caisar-ai`. Return to Cloudflare.
5. Pick the `caisar-ai` repo from the list. Click **Begin setup**.

- [ ] **Step 4: Configure build settings**

In **Set up builds and deployments**:
- **Project name:** `caisar-ai` (this becomes `caisar-ai.pages.dev`).
- **Production branch:** `main`.
- **Framework preset:** select **Astro** from the dropdown. This auto-fills:
  - **Build command:** `npm run build`
  - **Build output directory:** `dist`
  - **Root directory:** (leave blank)

Expand **Environment variables (advanced)** and add to **BOTH** the Production AND Preview scopes (this matters — there's a known Cloudflare bug where preview builds ignore production-only `NODE_VERSION`):
- `NODE_VERSION` = `22`

Click **Save and Deploy**.

- [ ] **Step 5: Watch the first build**

The first build runs immediately. Wait ~60 seconds. Expected: build succeeds, the project page shows a `*.pages.dev` URL.

Open the `*.pages.dev` URL. Expected: the site loads (same as the local prod preview). The theme toggle works. Code blocks are highlighted. No console errors.

- [ ] **Step 6: Verify preview deploys work**

Locally, push a trivial branch:
```bash
git checkout -b test-preview
echo "" >> README.md
git add README.md
git commit -m "test: trigger preview deploy"
git push -u origin test-preview
```

In the Cloudflare dashboard, watch the new `test-preview` deployment appear. Wait ~60s. Expected: a preview URL like `https://test-preview.caisar-ai.pages.dev` works.

Clean up:
```bash
git checkout main
git push origin --delete test-preview
git branch -D test-preview
```

## Task 21: Migrate caisar.ai DNS from Porkbun to Cloudflare

**Files:** none (manual UI; verification via `dig`)

⚠️ **Risk control before this task:** If the domain currently has MX records (email), they MUST be re-created in Cloudflare BEFORE flipping nameservers, or email goes down. Cloudflare's auto-scan usually catches them, but verify manually.

- [ ] **Step 1: Pre-flight — screenshot current Porkbun DNS records**

1. Log in to https://porkbun.com.
2. Click **ACCOUNT** (top right) → **Domain Management**.
3. Find `caisar.ai` → click **Details** (right side).
4. Take a screenshot of the current DNS records panel. Note any:
   - **MX** records (mail servers)
   - **TXT** records (especially `v=spf1...` SPF and `_dmarc...` DMARC)
   - **DKIM** records (CNAME or TXT under `<selector>._domainkey`)
   - Any other A/AAAA/CNAME records

This is your reference for Step 4. If there are no MX/email records (caisar.ai doesn't currently receive email), you can skip the email-specific checks — but still screenshot what's there.

- [ ] **Step 2: Disable Porkbun DNSSEC (CRITICAL)**

Still in the Porkbun **Details** panel for caisar.ai, look for **Porkbun DNSSEC** or **Registry DNSSEC**. If either is ON, **turn it OFF now**.

⚠️ If you flip nameservers while DNSSEC is active, the chain of trust breaks and validating resolvers return SERVFAIL — the domain effectively goes dark for many users. This is the single biggest failure mode for this migration.

- [ ] **Step 3: Add caisar.ai to Cloudflare**

1. In Cloudflare dashboard, left nav: **Domains** → **Onboard a domain** (the modern equivalent of "Add a site").
2. Enter `caisar.ai`. Click **Continue**.
3. Plan selection: scroll to the bottom and pick **Free**. Click **Continue**.
4. Cloudflare scans the current Porkbun nameservers and imports all detected records. Wait a few seconds for the scan to finish. You land on the DNS records table.

- [ ] **Step 4: Verify the imported DNS records line by line**

Open the screenshot from Step 1. Compare it against Cloudflare's imported table:
- Every **MX** record present with matching priority + target.
- Every **TXT** record present (especially `v=spf1` SPF and `_dmarc` DMARC).
- Every **DKIM** CNAME/TXT (e.g. `selector._domainkey.caisar.ai`).
- Any **A**/**AAAA**/**CNAME** records.

Add anything missing manually via Cloudflare's **Add record** button. Set MX and TXT records to **DNS only** (gray cloud) — they cannot be proxied. For apex A/AAAA and `www` CNAME, you can leave them proxied (orange cloud) or DNS-only; we'll override them in Task 22 anyway.

- [ ] **Step 5: Get the two Cloudflare nameservers**

Click **Continue to Activation** or open the zone **Overview** page. Cloudflare displays two assigned hostnames, e.g.:
- `xxx.ns.cloudflare.com`
- `yyy.ns.cloudflare.com`

Copy both. The hostnames are specific to your zone — they are NOT the same across Cloudflare accounts.

- [ ] **Step 6: Replace Porkbun nameservers with the Cloudflare ones**

1. Back in Porkbun → **ACCOUNT** → **Domain Management** → `caisar.ai` → **Details**.
2. Locate the **Nameservers** field. Click the **edit (pencil)** icon next to it.
3. In the pop-up, **delete the existing Porkbun nameservers** (e.g. `curitiba.ns.porkbun.com` etc.).
4. **Paste the two Cloudflare hostnames**, one per line, with no trailing dot and no extra whitespace.
5. Click **Save Nameservers**. Confirm if prompted.

- [ ] **Step 7: Trigger Cloudflare's activation check**

Return to the Cloudflare dashboard for caisar.ai. Click **Done, check nameservers** (or **Check nameservers** on the Overview page). Cloudflare polls periodically and emails you when the zone becomes Active.

- [ ] **Step 8: Verify propagation**

In a terminal:
```bash
dig +short NS caisar.ai
dig @1.1.1.1 +short NS caisar.ai
dig @8.8.8.8 +short NS caisar.ai
```

Expected (eventually — can take from minutes to ~24h, but usually <1h for `.ai`): all three queries return only the two `*.ns.cloudflare.com` hostnames.

Also verify email records still resolve:
```bash
dig +short MX caisar.ai
dig +short TXT caisar.ai
dig +short TXT _dmarc.caisar.ai
```

These should match what was at Porkbun before the swap.

Wait for the Cloudflare activation email AND for the dig output to be Cloudflare-only before continuing.

- [ ] **Step 9: Don't re-enable DNSSEC yet**

DNSSEC can be re-enabled later via Cloudflare → DNS → Settings → DNSSEC → Enable, then install the DS record at Porkbun. This is optional, can be done any time, and is out of scope for v1.

---

## Task 22: Add custom domain to Cloudflare Pages and enable Web Analytics

**Files:** none (manual UI)

- [ ] **Step 1: Add caisar.ai as a custom domain**

1. Cloudflare dashboard → **Workers & Pages** → click the `caisar-ai` project.
2. Click the **Custom domains** tab.
3. Click **Set up a custom domain**.
4. Enter `caisar.ai`. Click **Continue**.
5. Click **Activate domain**. Cloudflare auto-creates the CNAME on the zone (the apex uses CNAME flattening, which is why the zone has to be on Cloudflare DNS — which it now is, from Task 21).

⚠️ Do NOT manually pre-create a CNAME to `caisar-ai.pages.dev` in the DNS table before doing this — that triggers error 522. Always add the domain in the Pages UI first.

- [ ] **Step 2: Add www.caisar.ai**

Click **Set up a custom domain** again, enter `www.caisar.ai`. Click **Continue** → **Activate domain**. Cloudflare creates the `www → caisar-ai.pages.dev` CNAME automatically.

Wait until both domains show status **Active** (usually <1 min). SSL certs are auto-provisioned via Universal SSL.

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify the custom domain serves the site**

In a terminal:
```bash
curl -sI https://caisar.ai | head -20
curl -sI https://www.caisar.ai | head -20
```

Expected:
- `HTTP/2 200` on `caisar.ai` (or 301 to apex on `www`).
- `Server: cloudflare` header.
- `Content-Security-Policy` or related security headers.

In a browser, visit `https://caisar.ai`. Expected: the site loads over HTTPS with no certificate warnings. Theme toggle works.

- [ ] **Step 3b: Enable "Always Use HTTPS"**

In the Cloudflare dashboard for the caisar.ai zone → **SSL/TLS** → **Edge Certificates** → toggle **Always Use HTTPS** to ON. This 301-redirects any `http://` request to `https://`.

Verify:
```bash
curl -sI http://caisar.ai | grep -i location
```
Expected: a `location: https://caisar.ai/` redirect header.

- [ ] **Step 4: Enable Cloudflare Web Analytics**

1. Cloudflare dashboard left nav: **Analytics & Logs** → **Web Analytics** (sometimes listed simply as "Web Analytics").
2. Click **Add a site**.
3. From the hostname dropdown, choose `caisar.ai` (only zones already on Cloudflare DNS appear here — which is why we did Task 21 first).
4. Click **Done**.

Because caisar.ai is on Cloudflare DNS and proxied (orange-cloud), the default **Automatic setup** edge-injects the beacon script. No code change needed on the origin.

- [ ] **Step 5: Verify the beacon is being injected**

```bash
curl -s https://caisar.ai | grep -i 'cloudflareinsights.com/beacon'
```

Expected: a line containing `static.cloudflareinsights.com/beacon.min.js` is found. If not found:
- Make sure the apex DNS record is **Proxied** (orange cloud) in Cloudflare DNS.
- Make sure the origin does NOT send `Cache-Control: public, no-transform` (it shouldn't by default).
- Wait a few minutes — propagation can take a moment.

Visit a couple of pages on the live site. Within a few minutes, data starts appearing in Web Analytics → caisar.ai.

## Task 23: Acceptance criteria sign-off

**Files:** none (verification only)

Walk through every acceptance criterion from the spec (`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-07-caisar-ai-blog-design.md`). Confirm each one. If any fail, file follow-up tasks before declaring done.

- [ ] **Criterion 1:** `https://caisar.ai` resolves and serves the homepage over HTTPS with a valid TLS cert.

```bash
curl -sI https://caisar.ai | head -5
```

- [ ] **Criterion 2:** Homepage shows: name, bio, social/email links, and the 3 most recent posts.

Open https://caisar.ai in a browser. Verify visually.

- [ ] **Criterion 3:** `/writing` lists all non-draft posts in reverse chronological order with title, date, summary, and tags.

Open https://caisar.ai/writing/. Verify.

- [ ] **Criterion 4:** `/writing/<slug>` renders an MDX post with Shiki-highlighted code blocks and respects dark/light mode.

Open https://caisar.ai/writing/hello-world/. Toggle theme — confirm code block colors switch.

- [ ] **Criterion 5:** Theme toggle persists across page loads and does not flash incorrect theme on first paint.

In an Incognito window, set OS to dark mode, visit caisar.ai. Watch for any white flash on initial paint (there should be none). Toggle to light. Reload. Confirm light persists.

- [ ] **Criterion 6:** Lighthouse mobile score ≥95 on `/`, `/writing`, and a sample post.

DevTools → Lighthouse → Mobile → run on each URL. All three should show ≥95 on Performance, Accessibility, Best Practices, SEO.

- [ ] **Criterion 7:** At least one real post is live (not placeholder).

⚠️ **Blocks launch:** the plan scaffolds `hello-world.mdx` as a placeholder so the build has content to render during implementation. The spec is unambiguous that a *real* post must be live — rewrite or replace `hello-world.mdx` with an actual first essay before launch is complete. Shipping the placeholder does NOT satisfy this criterion.

- [ ] **Criterion 8:** Cloudflare Web Analytics receiving pageviews from the live domain.

In the Cloudflare dashboard → Web Analytics → caisar.ai. Confirm pageviews from your test visits appear within ~5 minutes.

- [ ] **Criterion 9:** Add-a-new-post workflow works end-to-end.

```bash
# In the repo root:
cat > src/content/posts/test-deploy.mdx <<'EOF'
---
title: "Deployment test"
summary: "Quick test that pushing a new file deploys within ~90 seconds."
pubDate: 2026-06-07
draft: false
---

This is a deployment timing test.
EOF

git add src/content/posts/test-deploy.mdx
git commit -m "test: deployment timing post"
git push origin main
```

Time the wait. Expected: post appears at https://caisar.ai/writing/test-deploy/ within ~90 seconds.

Cleanup after the test:
```bash
git rm src/content/posts/test-deploy.mdx
git commit -m "test: remove deployment timing post"
git push origin main
```

- [ ] **Criterion 10:** `sitemap-index.xml` and `robots.txt` are served and reference the canonical domain.

```bash
curl -s https://caisar.ai/robots.txt
curl -s https://caisar.ai/sitemap-index.xml
curl -s https://caisar.ai/sitemap-0.xml | head -30
```

Expected: robots.txt has `Sitemap: https://caisar.ai/sitemap-index.xml`. The sitemap files reference `caisar.ai` URLs.

Confirm `/404/` is NOT in `sitemap-0.xml`.

- [ ] **Final commit (if any cleanup):**

```bash
git push origin main
```

## Done criteria

The blog is "v1 done" when:

- All 10 spec acceptance criteria above are checked.
- The hello-world placeholder has been replaced with a real first post. (Per criterion 7, a placeholder does NOT count — a real essay must be live.)
- `public/og-default.png` is a real 1200×630 image (not missing/skipped) — every page references it for link previews.
- The accent color in `src/styles/global.css` (`--color-accent`) has been confirmed or changed from the muted-blue default.
- The GitHub repo visibility (public vs private) was a conscious decision in Task 19, not just the default.
- All **EDIT BEFORE LAUNCH** placeholder copy is replaced with real text:
  - Homepage `<title>` + `<meta description>` (`src/pages/index.astro`).
  - Homepage bio copy (`src/pages/index.astro` `<h1>` and `<p>` underneath).
  - `/writing` `<meta description>` + intro paragraph (`src/pages/writing/index.astro`).
  - LinkedIn URL (`https://www.linkedin.com/in/REPLACE-ME`).
  - GitHub URL (`https://github.com/REPLACE-ME`).
  - The X/Twitter (or other socials) decision — add the link(s) or consciously omit.
  - Email (already `cesar@caisar.ai`; verify).
  - Header site name (`src/components/Header.astro` — "Cesar" currently; rename if desired).

## Deferred for v2 (do NOT add to v1)

These are explicitly out of scope per the spec. Resist scope creep — capture as follow-ups instead.

- RSS feed (~10 lines via `@astrojs/rss` + a `src/pages/rss.xml.ts` file).
- Tag index pages (`/writing/tag/<tag>`).
- Search (Pagefind, ~50 lines).
- Newsletter signup.
- Comments (Giscus).
- `/about`, `/now`, `/projects` pages.
- Per-post OG images (Satori / `@vercel/og`).
- Smarter reading-time (remark plugin).
- Webmentions, microformats.
- Re-enable DNSSEC on caisar.ai via Cloudflare (optional security hardening; can be done any time later).
